pass1_value.note: Docs state Help Scout supports OAuth 2.0; legacy API Keys are still mentioned as supported for existing apps.

final_value.note: Help Scout Mailbox API authentication docs describe using OAuth 2.0 access tokens (including authorization_code and client_credentials grants). Help Scout also documents API Keys as a legacy authentication method for existing integrations (see https://developer.helpscout.com/mailbox-api/overview/api-keys/).

pass1_value.note: Developers can register apps in the Developer Dashboard and obtain client ID/secret for development; creating a test app implies self-serve credentials without human approval.

final_value.note: Getting Started for the Mailbox API instructs developers to create an app in the Help Scout Developer Portal to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret, indicating self-serve credential creation via the portal.

pass1_value.note: API Reference states a REST API covering Conversations, Customers, Mailboxes, Users, reports and webhooks; community SDKs exist but no official SDKs.

final_value.note: The official Mailbox API documentation presents a RESTful API with broad coverage (e.g., conversations, customers, mailboxes, users, reports, webhooks) under the Mailbox API docs.

pass1_value.note: Public REST docs and self-serve OAuth credentials exist, so integrations can be built today.

final_value.note: Official REST API documentation is publicly available and the Getting Started guide indicates developers can create an app in the Help Scout Developer Portal to obtain OAuth client credentials, so an integration can be built today.
